name: Hawaiian Butter Fish Seared Poke
recipeIngredient: 1 Inch cubed Hawaiian butter fish (approximately 2 pounds)
1 Tablespoon finely chopped scallions, green part only
1 Tablespoon Koon Chun sweetened black vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the ground coriander, nori flakes, curry powder, turmeric, sesame seeds, salt, and pepper.
2. Toss the cubed fish with the dry spice mix in a large mixing bowl. Marinate for 1 hour in the refrigerator.
3. After marinating, sear the fish until browned in a lightly oiled wok. Set aside to chill.
4. In a separate large bowl, combine the wakame, scallions, onion, daikon, seaweed, and chilled, browned fish cubes.
5. In a mixing bowl, combine Koon Chun, fish sauce, garlic, and sake. Slowly whisk in the combined curry and peanut oils.
6. Toss the wakame mixture with the Koon Chun dressing. Refrigerate for 1/2 hour.
7. Mix together the curry powder, bay leaf, and peanut oil in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer.
8. Chill, strain, and reserve.
9. Immediately before serving, mix cilantro with the chilled fish mixture.
10. Garnish with macadamia nuts and coconut flakes. Drizzle with curry oil.
Yield: 4 servings
